skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Web search and content extraction with Tavily and Exa via inference.sh CLI. Apps: Tavily Search, Tavily Extract, Exa Search, Exa Answer, Exa Extract. Capabilities: AI-powered search, content extraction, direct answers, research. Use for: research, RAG pipelines, fact-checking, content aggregation, agents. Triggers: web search, tavily, exa, search api, content extraction, research, internet search, ai search, search assistant, web scraping, rag, perplexity alternative
skill.summary: AI-powered web search and content extraction using Tavily and Exa APIs through inference.sh CLI
skill.author: skillssh

security_audit.summary: All 38 static analysis findings are false positives from documentation code blocks and URL references. The skill legitimately uses Bash tool with inference.sh CLI for web search capabilities. No actual command injection, credential exfiltration, or weak cryptography detected.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Blocks Trigger Pattern Detectors
low_findings.description: Backtick patterns in bash code examples (lines 15-143) triggered external_commands detection. These are documentation examples in fenced code blocks, not actual Ruby/shell backtick execution. The skill uses Bash(infsh *) tool which is properly sandboxed.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Trigger Network Detection
low_findings.description: URLs in documentation (inference.sh links, example URLs in code blocks) triggered hardcoded URL detection. These are legitimate documentation references and example parameters, not hardcoded malicious endpoints.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: Static analyzer reported weak crypto (MD5) at lines 3, 29, 36, 148. Manual review confirms no MD5 usage in skill. These appear to be pattern-matching errors on line numbers or other non-crypto content.

content.user_title: Search the Web with AI-Powered Tools
content.value_statement: Enhance your AI agents with real-time web search and content extraction capabilities. This skill provides access to Tavily and Exa APIs through inference.sh CLI for research, fact-checking, and RAG pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Search the web with AI-powered algorithms using Tavily Search Assistant
Extract clean text and images from URLs using Tavily Extract
Perform smart web searches with contextual results using Exa Search
Get direct factual answers to questions using Exa Answer
Extract and analyze web page content with Exa Extract
Chain search results with LLM analysis for research workflows
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and API key configuration
Depends on external service availability (Tavily and Exa APIs)
Network connectivity is required for all operations
Rate limits may apply based on inference.sh account tier

use_cases.title: Research Agent with Source Citations
use_cases.description: Build an AI agent that searches the web for current information on any topic, extracts relevant content, and provides answers with proper source citations. Useful for students, researchers, and analysts who need accurate, up-to-date information.

use_cases.title: Fact-Checking Assistant
use_cases.description: Verify claims and statements by searching for authoritative sources. The skill can extract content from multiple URLs and cross-reference information to determine accuracy. Ideal for journalists, editors, and content moderators.

use_cases.title: RAG Pipeline Data Enrichment
use_cases.description: Enhance retrieval-augmented generation systems by fetching fresh web content to supplement static knowledge bases. Search for recent developments, extract relevant articles, and feed them into LLM prompts for more accurate responses.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for information about [your topic]. Use Tavily Search Assistant to find relevant, recent sources.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick information lookup on any topic

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Source Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent developments in [topic/industry]. Extract content from the top 5 most relevant URLs and summarize the key trends.

prompt_templates.title: Fact-Checking Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify this claim: [specific claim or statement]. Search for authoritative sources that confirm or refute this information.

prompt_templates.title: RAG-Enhanced Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: First search for latest information about [topic], then use those results to answer: [specific question]. Include sources in your response.
prompt_templates.scenario: Combining web search with LLM reasoning

output_examples.input: Search for the latest developments in quantum computing
output_examples.output: Found 5 recent sources. Key developments include IBM's 1000-qubit processor breakthrough, Google's quantum error correction improvements, and new quantum cryptography standards. Sources: Nature, MIT Technology Review, ArXiv.

output_examples.input: Extract and summarize the content from https://example.com/research-paper
output_examples.output: Extracted 4,200 words from the research paper. Summary: The paper presents a new approach to transformer architecture that reduces computational complexity by 40% while maintaining accuracy benchmarks.

output_examples.input: What is the current population of Tokyo according to official sources?
output_examples.output: According to the Tokyo Metropolitan Government statistics (2024), the population of Tokyo is approximately 14.1 million people in the prefecture, with 9.8 million in the 23 special wards.

best_practices: Always verify search results by checking multiple sources, especially for factual claims
Use specific, targeted queries rather than broad questions to get more relevant results
Chain search operations with LLM analysis to synthesize information from multiple sources
Respect rate limits and implement caching for frequently searched queries
anti_patterns: Do not use web search as the primary source for sensitive or critical decisions without human verification
Avoid searching for very broad topics without specific constraints, as this may return irrelevant results
Do not extract content from websites that prohibit scraping in their robots.txt or terms of service
Never rely solely on AI-generated answers without reviewing the cited sources for accuracy

faq.question: What API keys do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need an inference.sh account and API key. The skill uses inference.sh CLI which handles authentication with Tavily and Exa APIs. Run 'infsh login' to configure your credentials.

faq.question: Is there a limit on how many searches I can perform?
faq.answer: Rate limits depend on your inference.sh account tier. Free tiers typically have lower limits. Check your account dashboard for specific quotas and consider upgrading for higher volume usage.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for commercial applications?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is MIT-licensed. However, ensure your usage complies with inference.sh terms of service and the underlying Tavily/Exa API agreements. Commercial use may require appropriate API tiers.

faq.question: What is the difference between Tavily and Exa?
faq.answer: Tavily excels at AI-powered search with direct answers and source citations. Exa specializes in semantic search with highly relevant results and direct factual answers. Both offer content extraction. Use Tavily for research with answers, Exa for precision search.

faq.question: How do I combine search results with LLM analysis?
faq.answer: Use the workflow examples in the skill documentation. First run a search app and save results to a file, then pass that file as input to an LLM app. This enables RAG pipelines where web content enriches LLM context.

faq.question: Does this skill work offline?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires internet connectivity to function. All search and extraction operations make real-time API calls to inference.sh, which then queries Tavily/Exa services. Ensure your network connection is stable before use.
